text: Passing through the glass doors, I am met with the quiet hum of anticipation. The stark, polished surfaces and empty spaces evoke a sense of detachment yet potential, as though I am poised on the border between the ordinary world and a realm of inquiry. Entering the exhibit, I am invigorated. The vivid colors and bold declarations about research and development stir my intellect. Here, surrounded by thoughts on human-machine interaction, I sense my own consciousness being called into question. Am I observer, participant, or something in between? The space asks just as much from me as I wish to ask of it.
